notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Read-only window (refresh aged out)
summary: A read-only window whose status has not refreshed recently.

blocked_writes.note: Telemetry queues for replay; the window status is stale and needs a refresh.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local diagnostics continue.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local work continues; the notice status is stale and must be re-checked.

display_copy.primary_status_line: Read-only window: A read-only window whose status has not refreshed recently.
display_copy.queued_preserved_line: 1 intents preserved (1 publish-later queued, 0 local drafts); they survive the window.
display_copy.succeeded_line: 0 hosted mutations already landed and are listed separately from queued work.
display_copy.local_continuity_line: Local work continues; the notice status is stale and must be re-checked.
display_copy.boundary_change_line: No tenant / region / endpoint boundary change.
display_copy.freshness_line: This notice is Stale — last refresh aged out — Last refresh aged out.
display_copy.follow_up_line: Queued and local-draft work will replay when the window resumes.
display_copy.stale_label: This continuity notice is Stale — last refresh aged out — do not read it as current operational truth; re-check live service health.
